answer:
MongoDB Atlas offers a perpetual free-forever M0 shared cluster tier that supports self-serve setup for development and prototyping [1][2][3]. The free tier provides 512MB of storage and includes full access to the MongoDB API, Atlas Search, and the Data API (a REST interface for database operations) [3].

You can manage your cluster using the Atlas Administration API for programmatic infrastructure control [4]. Integration is supported via the Atlas CLI, Go SDK, or direct REST requests to administrative endpoints [4].

Example of interacting with the Data API using cURL [3]:

```bash
curl -X POST "https://data.mongodb-api.com/app/YOUR_APP/endpoint/data/v1/action/findOne" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "api-key: YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -d '{
    "dataSource": "Cluster0",
    "database": "mydb",
    "collection": "users",
    "filter": {"email": "alex@example.com"}
  }'
```
